acceptance_criteria.text: An LLM-judge scoring pass rates answer quality on a defined rubric.
Manual spot-check harness allowing a human to compare judge scores against a sample of manually-rated answers for calibration.
pytest agents/eval/test_llm_judge.py (LLM-judge call mocked): scoring pipeline produces expected score structure.
a manual calibration script is runnable and produces a comparison report.
No live calls to OpenRouter/Gemini; all tests use mocked LLM clients.
covered_by: All test_llm_judge.py tests use _StubLLMClient(LLMClient) or httpx.MockTransport-backed OllamaClient -- zero real network, zero .env read

acceptance_criteria.text: Judge calls routed through LLMInterceptor.call(), not complete()/complete_with_usage() directly.
covered_by: llm_judge.score_answer's sole call path is interceptor.call(...)
test_llm_judge.py::test_score_answer_via_intercepted_ollama_mock_transport (asserts a real StageRecord is produced and is aggregate_by_stage-compatible)

acceptance_criteria.text: Judge response parsing never silently invents a score.

explicitly_deferred: Live calibration run against real OpenRouter/Gemini judge-model outputs -- deferred to a separate cost-estimated run, per binding scoping constraint.
Wiring score_arm_answers into agents/eval/run_benchmark.py (subtask 5.3.4, not yet built).
